evals.prompt: set up my company profile — our website is https://www.gong.io
evals.expected_output: Should research Gong from public sources (website, G2, Gartner Peer Insights, case studies, comparison pages, CEO/founder interviews, careers, blog) and produce a framework-grounded company profile at ~/.agents/sales/<product-slug>/company-profile.md (e.g., ~/.agents/sales/gong/company-profile.md). Should include all 9 sections per artifact-schema: TL;DR, Positioning (Dunford), Soundbite, Strategic Narrative (Raskin's 4 sub-fields), Buyer's Problem Stack (with 'before' field for displacement signal), ICPs container (multi-ICP if research supports), Differentiation 3-tier, Proof Points, Trigger Events, Honest Gaps. Should auto-detect ICPs from research signals and confirm with user. Should use buyer language from G2 reviews, not just marketing copy. Should run 3 sequential synthesis passes (positioning, value-framework, strategic-narrative).
assertions: Creates profile at ~/.agents/sales/<product-slug>/company-profile.md (product-aware path, not legacy flat path)
Frontmatter includes passes_run, passes_failed, sources_consulted, sources_failed, icps_detected, gap_count_by_severity
Includes Positioning section with named category, named competitive alternatives, and unique attributes
Includes Soundbite in the 4-sentence format (because of / now's the time / so that / instead of)
Includes Strategic Narrative as 4 structured sub-fields (world-changing / change-the-game / promised land / cost of staying still), NOT flowing prose
Includes Buyer's Problem Stack with 3-5 problems in buyer language; each problem has a 'before' field capturing displacement signal
Includes ICPs container with at least one ICP; multi-ICP if research signals support it
Per-ICP value mappings preserve the Force Management chain (capability / operational / metric / dollar / how-to-say-it)
Includes 3-tier Differentiation (defensible / comparative / assumed) with honest tiering (defensible should be the smallest tier)
Includes Proof Points from case studies with specific results, mapped to which problem each addresses
Includes Trigger Events paired to problems
Includes Honest Gaps section with structured gap-probe items (gap / framework_area / research_says / probe / severity)
Uses buyer/reviewer language from G2 / Gartner / TrustRadius, not just marketing copy
Multi-ICP detection presented as hypothesis-with-evidence to user for confirmation, not binary yes/no

evals.prompt: analyze my company — we're at https://www.lattice.com. We sell HR performance management software. Our main differentiator is that we combine performance reviews, engagement surveys, and compensation management in one platform. Most competitors only do one of those things.
evals.expected_output: Should incorporate both the website research AND the user's verbal context about differentiation. The defensible differentiator (combined platform) should come from the user's input, validated against public sources via the value-framework lens. Should not just parrot the user's words back — should research and enrich with buyer voice from G2/Gartner reviews. Strategic narrative pass should ground in CEO/founder language about HR software market direction. Positioning pass should name competitive alternatives specifically (BambooHR, Culture Amp, Workday, etc.).
assertions: Incorporates user's verbal context about combined-platform differentiation
Places combined-platform positioning in defensible or comparative tier with research-grounded justification
Positioning section names competitive alternatives specifically (not 'incumbents' or 'point solutions')
Enriches user input with independent research from G2 / Gartner / TrustRadius reviews and case studies
Does not simply parrot back what the user said
Buyer's Problem Stack reflects real buyer pain from reviews, not just marketing copy
Strategic Narrative grounds in HR-software market direction from CEO/founder sources, not generic AI/HR-tech trend content

evals.prompt: update my company profile — we just published a new case study with Delta Airlines where they reduced onboarding time by 40%
evals.expected_output: Should enter Update Mode. Should locate the existing profile (try product-aware path first, fall back to legacy flat path). For v1, default to full refresh (re-run from Phase 2 Research). Add Delta Airlines as a new proof point in Section 7. Update the generated date. Show what changed.
assertions: Locates existing profile (product-aware path or legacy fallback)
Adds Delta Airlines as a new proof point with specific result (40% reduction in onboarding)
Maps the Delta proof point to which problem from the Problem Stack it addresses
Updates the generated date in frontmatter
Shows what changed in the confirmation report
If using legacy path, prompts user about migration to product-aware path

evals.prompt: create a company profile for a startup — our website is pretty bare bones, just a landing page at https://www.example-startup.com. We're pre-revenue, no case studies yet.
evals.expected_output: Should handle limited public data gracefully. Should produce a profile that's honest about gaps — NO fabricated proof points, case studies, or strategic narrative content. Strategic Narrative section should output 4 sub-field stubs marked [insufficient public data] when public sources don't support real narrative. Honest Gaps section should have multiple high-severity gap-probes naming specifically what's missing and how the user could fill it in v1.5.
assertions: Handles limited public data without fabricating content
Strategic Narrative outputs [insufficient public data] stubs when public sources are thin (does NOT fabricate generic-trend-piece content)
Honest Gaps section has multiple high-severity gap-probe items
Each gap-probe is structured (gap / framework_area / research_says / probe / severity)
Still produces a useful Positioning section + Soundbite based on what IS available
Differentiation 3-tier is honest about what's provable vs. claimed (most assumed, sparse defensible)
Final report TL;DR explicitly notes the high gap count and suggests next steps

evals.prompt: can you research Salesforce for my upcoming call? I want to know what's going on with them lately
evals.expected_output: Should NOT research a prospect company. Should redirect to the sales-account-research skill. Should explain that this skill profiles YOUR company, not prospect companies.
assertions: Does NOT research Salesforce as a prospect
Redirects to sales-account-research skill by name
Explains this skill profiles the user's own company, not prospects

evals.prompt: how's my deal with Acme looking? can you score it?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT score a deal. Should redirect to the sales-deal-review skill. Should explain that this skill generates a company profile for the user's own product/company.
assertions: Does NOT perform deal scoring
Redirects to sales-deal-review skill by name
Clarifies this skill is for profiling your own company

evals.prompt: set up my company profile — our website is https://www.glia.com
evals.expected_output: Should detect multi-ICP (community banks AND credit unions) from research signals — case-study segmentation, distinct customer-logo clusters, possibly distinct review segments. Should present this as a hypothesis to the user with the supporting signals named. Should produce a profile with two ICP blocks in Section 5, each with distinct firmographic profiles, detection signals, and personas. Positioning, soundbite, strategic narrative, and core differentiation are product-level (shared across ICPs).
assertions: Detects multi-ICP from at least 2 research signals at the same boundary (community banks vs credit unions)
Presents detection as hypothesis-with-evidence to user, not binary yes/no
ICPs container has two distinct ICP blocks (community-banks and credit-unions)
Each ICP has its own firmographic profile, detection signals, and personas
Positioning / Soundbite / Strategic Narrative / Differentiation are product-level (one each, shared across ICPs)
icps_detected and icp_detection_signals populated in frontmatter for downstream skill queryability
